title: Forwarder Connectivity
spl:
index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd group=tcpin_connections
| stats latest(_time) as last_seen by hostname, sourceIp
| eval hours_since=round((now()-last_seen)/3600,1)
| where hours_since > 1
| table hostname, sourceIp, hours_since
| sort -hours_since
description: Silent forwarder failures mean data gaps that may not be noticed until an investigation fails. Detection ensures data completeness.
value: Silent forwarder failures mean data gaps that may not be noticed until an investigation fails. Detection ensures data completeness.
implementation: Track last-seen timestamp per forwarder from `_internal`. Alert when any forwarder hasn't reported in >1 hour. Maintain forwarder inventory for coverage analysis. Cross-reference with host downtime events.
visualization: Table (silent forwarders), Single value (forwarders reporting), Status grid (forwarder √ó health), Bar chart (silent by location).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Monitoring Console, Deployment Monitor app.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `_internal` (metrics.log ‚Äî tcpin_connections).
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Track last-seen timestamp per forwarder from `_internal`. Alert when any forwarder hasn't reported in >1 hour. Maintain forwarder inventory for coverage analysis. Cross-reference with host downtime events.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd group=tcpin_connections
| stats latest(_time) as last_seen by hostname, sourceIp
| eval hours_since=round((now()-last_seen)/3600,1)
| where hours_since > 1
| table hostname, sourceIp, hours_since
| sort -hours_since
```

Understanding this SPL

**Forwarder Connectivity** ‚Äî Silent forwarder failures mean data gaps that may not be noticed until an investigation fails. Detection ensures data completeness.

Documented **Data sources**: `_internal` (metrics.log ‚Äî tcpin_connections). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Monitoring Console, Deployment Monitor app. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: _internal; **sourcetype**: splunkd. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=_internal, sourcetype=splunkd. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by hostname, sourceIp** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **hours_since** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where hours_since > 1` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ Pipeline stage (see **Forwarder Connectivity**): table hostname, sourceIp, hours_since
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Run the search over calm and busy windows. Compare with the Splunk monitoring console and relevant health and introspection views, and spot-check key fields in sample events.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (silent forwarders), Single value (forwarders reporting), Status grid (forwarder √ó health), Bar chart (silent by location).
grandmaExplanation: We watch whether data forwarders stay connected so our log and metric streams do not go quiet by surprise.
knownFalsePositives: Lag during indexer maintenance, network throttling, or scheduled config syncs. We check deployment health before we blame one agent.
